bugs.root_cause: Code path had no error handling — exceptions would propagate uncaught
bugs.fix: Added try/catch block

bugs.root_cause: Property access on potentially null/undefined value
bugs.fix: Added null safety (optional chaining or null check) | Also: if (!token) return;; let unsubscribe: (() => void) | undefined;

bugs.error_message: @mention notifications never arrive in real time (bell badge/dropdown never updates without a manual refresh)
bugs.root_cause: subscribeNotifications() had a registration-order race: the STOMP SUBSCRIBE for /user/queue/notifications was only ever sent from inside wsConnect's onConnect handler, gated on pendingNotificationCallbacks being non-empty at that exact instant. Every real caller (app-shell.tsx) awaited wsConnect(token) first and registered its callback after, so onConnect always ran with an empty queue, skipped the subscribe, and left activeNotificationSub null. subscribeNotifications() then fell into a branch that only pushed the callback into an array and returned — it never called stompClient.subscribe(). The backend's convertAndSendToUser(username, "/queue/notifications", ...) was correct but landed on a destination nobody had subscribed to.
bugs.fix: Rewrote subscribeNotifications() to always register the callback AND create the STOMP subscription immediately if stompClient.connected && !activeNotificationSub (covers the connected-but-registered-late case). Unsubscribe/cleanup now tears down activeNotificationSub only when the callback list is empty.

bugs.error_message: Real-time comment subscription leaks on task navigation and is fragile under React StrictMode
bugs.root_cause: The comment-subscription useEffect captured the unsubscribe function returned by subscribeTaskComments(...) inside the wsConnect(token).then(...) callback and returned it from there, not from the effect itself — so React never called it on cleanup. Combined with a wsInitialized ref that the cleanup reset (rather than gating the effect), the underlying STOMP subscription was left dangling on every task unmount/remount (e.g. StrictMode double-invoke or navigating away and back).
bugs.fix: Hoisted an outer `let unsubscribe` variable, assigned it inside the .then() callback, and called `unsubscribe?.()` from the effect's real cleanup alongside a `cancelled` flag. Removed the wsInitialized ref guard entirely.

bugs.error_message: Notification dropdown 'next page' is a no-op
bugs.root_cause: getRecentNotifications() was typed as () => Promise<NotificationResponse[]> and took no arguments, but /notifications/recent actually returns a Spring Page, and app-shell.tsx called getRecentNotifications(page, 20) and read .content/.totalPages off the result. The page argument was silently dropped (never sent as a query param), so it happened to work for page 0 only.
bugs.fix: Added a Page<T> interface and changed getRecentNotifications to accept (page = 0, size = 20), sending them as ?page=&size= query params, returning Promise<Page<NotificationResponse>>.

bugs.root_cause: 2 lines replaced/restructured
bugs.fix: Rewrote 7→8 lines (2 removed)

bugs.root_cause: data: comments, isLoading: commentsLoading → comments, isLoading: commentsLoading, isError: commentsError
bugs.fix: Changed data: comments, isLoading: commentsLoading → comments, isLoading: commentsLoading, isError: commentsError

bugs.error_message: Members not in a project's team_members list get HTTP 500 (not 403) when fetching a task or its comments, and the frontend silently shows an empty comment list instead of any error — reproduced with member@terra.com on task 17 (project 'Website Redesign', team_id=1 has 0 rows in team_members)
bugs.root_cause: Three compounding issues: (1) GlobalExceptionHandler's catch-all @ExceptionHandler(RuntimeException.class) intercepted AccessDeniedException (a RuntimeException subclass) and returned 500 instead of 403. (2) TaskController.getTaskById used a loose @PreAuthorize("hasRole('ADMIN') or hasRole('MANAGER') or hasRole('MEMBER')") with no task/project-specific check, unlike CommentController and TaskController.moveTask which both use @authorizationService.hasTaskAccess(...) — so a member with zero project access could still load the task detail page. (3) The taskComments useQuery in both task-detail routes had no error handling, so any fetch failure silently rendered the 'no comments yet' empty state, completely hiding the failure.
bugs.fix: Added @ExceptionHandler(AccessDeniedException.class) -> 403 in GlobalExceptionHandler (Spring resolves by most-specific type, so this doesn't require reordering the catch-all). Changed TaskController.getTaskById's @PreAuthorize to @authorizationService.hasTaskAccess(#principal.username, #id), matching moveTask/CommentController. Added isError (with retry:false) to the taskComments useQuery in both task-detail routes and rendered an Arabic access-error message instead of the silent empty state. Verified live: member@terra.com now gets 403 (was 500) on GET /api/member/tasks/17 and /comments; manager@terra.com (real access) unaffected and real-time comment delivery via WebSocket still works.

bugs.root_cause: Code path had no error handling — exceptions would propagate uncaught
bugs.fix: Added try/catch block

bugs.error_message: Member sees manager's plain (non-@mention) comments in real time only after posting/receiving an @mention first — reported as 'real-time comments only work with mentions'
bugs.root_cause: @stomp/stompjs v7 does not auto-resubscribe topics after a dropped connection reconnects (reconnectDelay: 5000 configured). Only the notification subscription (/user/queue/notifications) was revived inside onConnect. The per-task comment subscription (/topic/task/{taskId}/comments), created once via subscribeTaskComments() in a React effect, was never re-created on reconnect. After any reconnect (heartbeat timeout, network blip, backend restart) the comment subscription was silently dead while the notification subscription (and therefore @mention delivery) kept working — making it look like only mentions ever deliver live.
bugs.fix: Added a commentSubs registry (taskId, callback, sub) mirroring the existing notification-callback pattern. createCommentStompSub() creates/recreates a subscription from a registry entry. onConnect now iterates commentSubs and recreates every subscription on every (re)connect. subscribeTaskComments() registers into commentSubs and creates the subscription immediately if already connected (no longer a no-op warning when called pre-connect); its returned unsubscribe both unsubscribes and removes the registry entry. wsDisconnect() resets commentSubs = []. Frontend-only change; backend already broadcasts every comment unconditionally (CommentService.addComment). No changes needed in the task-detail routes — they already clean up via the returned unsubscribe (bug-004).

bugs.error_message: Member viewing a task detail page sees the manager's role label ('مدير المشروع') and manager-only sidebar links (لوحة التحكم، إنشاء مشروع، التحليلات) instead of member-appropriate navigation
bugs.root_cause: All 3 <AppShell persona="manager"> call sites in this file are hardcoded to "manager" — almost certainly copy-pasted from the manager route's tasks.$taskId.tsx without updating the persona prop. localStorage terra_user correctly reports role: MEMBER; this is a display-only bug in the shared AppShell prop, not an access-control issue (backend @PreAuthorize/hasTaskAccess checks are unaffected and independently enforced).
bugs.fix: FIXED (2026-07-06, together with bug-014): changed persona="manager" to persona="member" at all 3 call sites in member/tasks.$taskId.tsx.

bugs.root_cause: 2 lines replaced/restructured
bugs.fix: Rewrote 4→5 lines (2 removed)

bugs.error_message: Comments are real-time on the manager side but NOT on the member side
bugs.root_cause: Two compounding issues, both frontend (backend broadcasts /topic/task/{id}/comments unconditionally and the WS interceptor only authenticates CONNECT — no role gating). (1) member/tasks.$taskId.tsx — the ONLY member page that calls subscribeTaskComments — has beforeLoad: requireRole("/manager") copy-pasted from the manager route. requireRole compares the prefix to rolePrefixMap[role]; for a MEMBER, "/manager" !== "/member" → redirect to /member/kanban. So actual members can never reach the page with the working WS subscription (same copy-paste family as bug-011 persona="manager"). (2) The places members actually read comments — the task modals in member/kanban.tsx and member/projects.tsx — fetch via plain useQuery(getTaskComments) and only invalidateQueries after the member posts their OWN comment. Neither file imports anything from lib/websocket.ts, so incoming comments from the manager never appear until the modal is reopened or the member posts.
bugs.fix: FIXED (2026-07-06): (a) changed requireRole("/manager") to requireRole("/member") in member/tasks.$taskId.tsx (persona prop fixed alongside, see bug-011), and (b) added a wsConnect + subscribeTaskComments effect (mirroring the manager route's bug-004 cleanup pattern, gated on the modal's `open` prop) to the TaskDetailDialog components in member/kanban.tsx and member/projects.tsx, updating the ["taskComments", taskId] query cache on incoming comment/delete events. Verified with tsc --noEmit and eslint against the pre-edit baseline — no new type or lint errors introduced.

bugs.error_message: Header search state placed in SearchContext.Provider inside AppShell would never be visible to page components (query always empty, setQuery a no-op)
bugs.root_cause: Page route components render <AppShell persona="...">{content}</AppShell> — the page is AppShell's PARENT in the fiber tree, not its child. A Provider placed inside AppShell (wrapping {children}) is a descendant of the page, so useContext() called in the page itself can never see it — it silently falls back to the Context default value. Caught during self-review before considering the task done, by reasoning through the actual component tree rather than trusting that tsc passing meant it worked.
bugs.fix: Moved SearchContext state (and the reset-on-route-change effect) up to AppContent in frontend/src/routes/__root.tsx, a genuine ancestor of every routed page. AppShell now just consumes the same context via useContext instead of owning/providing it.
